tactical_shape: Front-foot attacking play with João Cancelo reaching the byline and Nuno Mendes delivering from wide areas
Set-piece heavy approach, with rehearsed free-kick and corner routines emphasized
High starting position for Cristiano Ronaldo in the second half to hunt goals
standout_players: Cristiano Ronaldo — scored twice, ended his major-tournament goal drought, and looked relentless
Nuno Mendes — provided the free-kick goal with a disguised run and whipped finish
João Cancelo — supplied the byline cutback for Ronaldo's opener and another delivery that almost produced a hat-trick
Bruno Fernandes — central to the second and third goals, exploiting space and delivering set-piece quality
Rafael Leão — scored the final goal with a rising drive
Austin McPhee — credited for Portugal's inventive set-piece work
fatigue_injuries: Cristiano Ronaldo took a blow late on but continued
home_read.momentum: Portugal looked rampant and inventive, with Ronaldo back among the goals and the side showing a dangerous set-piece edge going forward.

tactical_shape: Utterly outclassed defensively, unable to cope with Portugal's standard
Briefly rallied after going behind but were largely overwhelmed
standout_players: Abdukodir Khusanov — made a dramatic intervention to deny Ronaldo a hat-trick and later was involved in the own-goal sequence
Abduvokhid Nematov — beaten by Portugal's set-pieces and could only paw the third goal over the line
Odiljon Khamrobekov — conceded the foul that led to Portugal's second goal
Azizjon Ganiev — had a blast disallowed
away_read.momentum: A chastening, one-sided defeat that underlined how far they were from Portugal's level.
